title: Phase 32.3A — Bing/IndexNow Execution Decision

current_state.bing_webmaster_tools: UNKNOWN — requires Bing WMT account verification

decision: SKIPPED_FOR_RISK — Cannot submit to IndexNow without key setup. Key setup requires either (a) Bing Webmaster Tools account + API key, or (b) manual key file generation.

analysis.blocker: No IndexNow API key exists. Generating a key requires creating a Bing Webmaster Tools account for qfinhub.com, which needs domain ownership verification (DNS TXT record or XML file upload).
analysis.cannot_automate_fully: Domain verification in Bing WMT requires either DNS access or file upload to production. Hermes has file upload access but DNS access is unknown.
analysis.setup_path: If Qasem has Bing WMT access: (1) Add qfinhub.com to Bing WMT, (2) Verify via XML file upload to public/, (3) Generate IndexNow API key, (4) Save key to .env.local as INDEXNOW_KEY, (5) Submit 6 URLs via curl POST to api.indexnow.org
analysis.alternative: Without Bing WMT: create a self-generated UUID key file at public/indexnow-key.txt with a randomly generated key. IndexNow protocol accepts self-generated keys — the key just needs to be a valid UUID v4 hosted at the root. Submit URLs via POST to api.indexnow.org with that key.

recommendation: PENDING_WITHOUT_MANUAL_DEPENDENCY — Hermes can generate a UUID key, place it at public/indexnow-key.txt, and submit the 6 URLs via curl to IndexNow API. This is fully automated and requires zero manual work.
